LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED. PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA MOTION TO ENFORCE STIPULATION INJUNCTION. GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS HAVE SHOWN THAT DEFENDANTS WILLFULLY VIOLATED THE STIPULATED INJUNCTION ENTERED ON OCTOBER 11, 2011. DEFENDANTS ARE ORDERED TO IMMEDIATELY COMPLY WITH ALL TERMS OF THE STIPULATED INJUNCTION WITH DOCUMENTARY PROOF OF COMPLIANCE PROVIDED TO PLAINTIFFS. DEFENDANTS ARE ALSO ENCOURAGED TO CONTINUE THEIR EFFORTS TO SELL THE PROPERTY. PLAINTIFFS ARE ENTITLED TO PAYMENT OF $11,600 FOR THEIR ATTORNEY'S FEES WHICH MUST BE PAID WITHIN 60 DAYS OR THROUGH AN ESCROW FOR THE SALE OF THE BUILDING, WHICHEVER OCCURS EARLIER. DEFENDANTS ARE FURTHER ORDERED TO PAY PLAINTIFFS $100,000 IN CIVIL PENALTIES FOR VIOLATING THE STIPULATED INJUNCTION. HOWEVER, THE $100,000 IS STAYED FOR 90 DAYS PENDING DEFENDANTS' SUCCESSFUL EFFORTS TO SELL THE BUILDING AND THEIR DOCUMENTED COMPLIANCE WITH ALL THE TERMS OF THE STIPULATED INJUNCTION. IF THE PROPERTY HAS BEEN SOLD OR IS IN ESCROW WITH ALL CONDITIONS WAIVED AND/OR DEFENDANTS ARE IN FULL COMPLIANCE WITH THE STIPULATED INJUNCTION WITHIN 60 DAYS, THE CIVIL PENALTIES WILL BE PERMANENTLY STAYED. IF THE PARTIES DISAGREE WHETHER DEFENDANTS ARE IN FULL COMPLIANCE WITH THE STIPULATED INJUNCTION, THE COURT, UPON EX PARTE APPLICATION AFTER THE PARTIES HAVE MET AND CONFERRED, WILL APPOINT A REFEREE TO DECIDE THAT ISSUE IN THE FIRST INSTANCE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976

ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS ARE DIRECTED TO PRODUCE FURTHER RESPONSES BY SEPTEMBER 16, 2011. THE COURT TOOK THE ISSUE OF SANCTIONS UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; NOT REPORTED -- AFTER HEARING THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $500. DEFENDANTS ARE DIRECTED TO REMIT THIS AMOUNT TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL BY SEPTEMBER 16, 2011. REQUEST FOR ISSUE AND EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS DENIED. PARTIES NOTIFIED OF THE COURT'S RULING BY TELEPHONE. PLAINTIFFS ARE DIRECTED TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.

Off Calendar Apr-25-2012 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Stipulatuion Injunction
Off Calendar May-02-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jill L. Cannon, DCA, from The Office of the City Attorney, 415-554-3820, for Plaintiff and moving party
Steven Adair MacDonald, Esq., from Steven Adair MacDonal & Associates, P.C., 415-956-6488 x-11, for Defendant and opposing party
Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified.
PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA Motion To Enforce Stipulation Injunction. Granted. Plaintiffs have shown that Defendants willfully violated the Stipulated Injunction entered on October 11, 2011. Defendants are ordered to immediately comply with all terms of the Stipulated Injunction with documentary proof of compliance provided to Plaintiffs. Defendants are also encouraged to continue their efforts to sell the property. Plaintiffs are entitled to payment of $11,600 for their attorney's fees which must be paid within 60 days or through an escrow for the sale of the building, whichever occurs earlier. Defendants are further ordered to pay Plaintiffs $100,000 in civil penalties for violating the Stipulated Injunction. However, the $100,000 is stayed for 90 days pending defendants' successful efforts to sell the building and their documented compliance with all the terms of the Stipulated Injunction. If the property has been sold or is in escrow with all conditions waived and/or defendants are in full compliance with the Stipulated Injunction within 60 days, the civil penalties will be permanently stayed. If the parties disagree whether defendants are in full compliance with the Stipulated Injunction, the court, upon ex parte application after the parties have met and conferred, will appoint a referee to decide that issue in the first instance. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976

MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AGAINST DEFENDANTS JAMES P QUINN AND PATRICIA D QUINN.
Appearances: Jerry Threet of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3914) for plaintiffs and moving parties; James Quinn (863.9334) defendant and responding party appearing in propria persona.
Argued and the court modified its ruling as follows: Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories, special interrogatories and request for production of documents is granted. Defendants are directed to produce further responses by September 16, 2011. The court took the issue of sanctions under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported
---
After hearing the court ruled as follows: Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions is granted in the amount of $500. Defendants are directed to remit this amount to plaintiff's counsel by September 16, 2011. Request for issue and evidentiary sanctions denied. Parties notified of the court's ruling by telephone. Plaintiffs are directed to submit a proposed form of order.
